CREATE TABLE test_strings (raw_string VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES ('ardèch'), ('café'), ('naïve'), ('exposé'), ('façade'), ('soufflé'), ('résumé');
CREATE TABLE normalization_results AS SELECT raw_string, nfc_normalize(raw_string) AS normalized FROM test_strings;
SELECT raw_string, normalized FROM normalization_results WHERE normalized IS NOT NULL;
SELECT raw_string, normalized FROM normalization_results WHERE normalized <> raw_string;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM normalization_results WHERE normalized LIKE '%é%';
SELECT DISTINCT normalized FROM normalization_results ORDER BY normalized;
CREATE TABLE additional_strings (id INT, more_strings VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO additional_strings VALUES (1, 'déjà'), (2, 'soupçon'), (3, 'cliché');

SELECT ts.raw_string, nfc_normalize(ts.raw_string) AS normalized_string, asb.more_strings FROM test_strings ts JOIN additional_strings asb ON nfc_normalize(ts.raw_string) LIKE '%' || nfc_normalize(asb.more_strings) || '%';
CREATE TABLE nested_tests AS SELECT nfc_normalize(SUBSTR(raw_string, 1, 5)) AS short_normalized FROM test_strings;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nested_tests WHERE short_normalized IN (SELECT nfc_normalize('café') AS comparison_value);
SELECT raw_string FROM test_strings WHERE nfc_normalize(raw_string) NOT IN (SELECT normal_value FROM (SELECT nfc_normalize('exposé') AS normal_value));
CREATE TABLE final_results AS SELECT nfc_normalize(raw_string) AS fully_normalized_string FROM test_strings GROUP BY fully_normalized_string HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT * FROM final_results WHERE fully_normalized_string LIKE 'façade%';
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_strings;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS additional_strings;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS normalization_results;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS nested_tests;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS final_results;
